repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
- fix https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39208
- fix generation of neste `@inlinedoc` classes

comment_data.body:
Will eventually lift this into a module scope once the "provider" story gets more articulated. Also did some rewording below. @echasnovski 

Also to avoid clumsy repetitive warning, I am here trying to establish "the picker" as a concept instead of repeating `the vim.ui.select implementation` everywhere.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5175,6 +5175,11 @@ vim.ui.select({items}, {opts}, {on_choice})                  *vim.ui.select()*
     Prompts the user to pick from a list of items, allowing arbitrary
     (potentially asynchronous) work until `on_choice`.
 
+    Plugins may override `vim.ui.select` to provide a custom "picker"
+    interface; they are expected to call the `format_item` and `preview_item`
+    handlers (if any) provided by the caller. They may also use the `kind`
+    hint (if provided by the caller) to decide how to handle some items.

comment_data.body: I think starting these with `Decides how to...` is a good pattern that signals to the reader that these provide behavior. Can revisit later if needed
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5196,31 +5201,31 @@ vim.ui.select({items}, {opts}, {on_choice})                  *vim.ui.select()*
 
     Parameters: ~
       • {items}      (`any[]`) Arbitrary items
-      • {opts}       (`table`) Additional options
-                     • {prompt}? (`string`) Text of the prompt. Defaults to
-                       `Select one of:`
-                     • {format_item}? (`fun(item: any):string`) Function to
-                       format an individual item from `items`. Defaults to
-                       `tostring`.
-                     • {preview_item}? (`fun(item: any):table`) Function to
-                       preview an individual item from `items`. If missing, no
-                       special preview is used. Should ensure a buffer with
-                       contents (text, highlighting) providing details about
-                       an item. Should return a table with information that
-                       `vim.ui.select` implementations may use to show the
-                       preview buffer in the way they see fit:
-                       • {buf}? (`integer`) - buffer id with preview. If
-                         missing, no preview should be shown.
-                       • {pos}? (`[integer, integer]`) - (1,0)-indexed tuple
-                         of where to position cursor in the preview buffer. If
-                         missing, should be treated as `{ 1, 0 }`.
+      • {opts}       (`table`) Options
+                     • {prompt}? (`string`, default: `"Select one of:"`)
+                       Prompt text.
+                     • {format_item}? (`fun(item: any):string`, default:
+                       `tostring`) Decides how to format items when displayed
+                       in the picker.
+                     • {preview_item}?
+                       (`fun(item: any):{buf?:integer, pos?:[integer,integer]}`)
+                       Decides how to preview an item by preparing a scratch

comment_data.body:
Rewording seems nice!

@justinmk, based on [this](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37360#issuecomment-4320139883), I'd also add the following:

- `--- Default implementation doesn't currently support preview.`
-
    ```lua
    ---     - {pos_end}? (`[integer, integer]`) - (1,0)-indexed tuple with an exclusive end of
    ---       a previewed range. If missing, no range is intended for a preview.
    ```

Would you do it in this PR (probably easier) or should I create a separate one?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5175,6 +5175,11 @@ vim.ui.select({items}, {opts}, {on_choice})                  *vim.ui.select()*
     Prompts the user to pick from a list of items, allowing arbitrary
     (potentially asynchronous) work until `on_choice`.
 
+    Plugins may override `vim.ui.select` to provide a custom "picker"
+    interface; they are expected to call the `format_item` and `preview_item`
+    handlers (if any) provided by the caller. They may also use the `kind`
+    hint (if provided by the caller) to decide how to handle some items.

comment_data.body:
If [`vim.Range` is better](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37360#issuecomment-4320240448), then something like this, maybe:

```diff
diff --git a/runtime/lua/vim/ui.lua b/runtime/lua/vim/ui.lua
index 0a62ee2356..beb748a5a2 100644
--- a/runtime/lua/vim/ui.lua
+++ b/runtime/lua/vim/ui.lua
@@ -13,9 +13,12 @@ local M = {}
 ---
 --- Decides how to preview an item by preparing a scratch buffer with the item details (including text, highlighting, etc.).
 --- When the picker decides to "preview" an item, it should call this function.
+--- Default implementation doesn't currently support preview.
 --- Must return a table with these keys:
 ---     - {buf}? (`integer`) - Buffer containing the previewed item details, or nil if no preview should be shown.
----     - {pos}? (`[integer, integer]`) - Specifies the (1,0)-indexed cursor position in the preview buffer. If nil, should be treated as `{ 1, 0 }`.
+---     - {range}? (`vim.Range`) - Specifies the range intended to preview.
+---       May be empty to only preview a position.
+---       If nil, should be treated as `vim.range(0, 0, 0, 0)`.
 ---@field preview_item? fun(item: any):{buf?:integer, pos?:[integer,integer]}
 ---
 --- Arbitrary hint string indicating the item shape. The picker may wish to use this to infer the
```

One slight problem with asking for `vim.Range` is that it will limit `vim.ui.select` implementations for supporting `preview_item` on older Nvim versions. But that is not necessarily a bad thing, since this is backported to 0.12.3.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -5175,6 +5175,11 @@ vim.ui.select({items}, {opts}, {on_choice})                  *vim.ui.select()*
     Prompts the user to pick from a list of items, allowing arbitrary
     (potentially asynchronous) work until `on_choice`.
 
+    Plugins may override `vim.ui.select` to provide a custom "picker"
+    interface; they are expected to call the `format_item` and `preview_item`
+    handlers (if any) provided by the caller. They may also use the `kind`
+    hint (if provided by the caller) to decide how to handle some items.

@@ -3,32 +3,35 @@ local M = {}
 ---@class vim.ui.select.Opts
 ---@inlinedoc
 ---
---- Text of the prompt. Defaults to `Select one of:`
+--- Prompt text.
+--- (default: `"Select one of:"`)
 ---@field prompt? string
 ---
---- Function to format an
---- individual item from `items`. Defaults to `tostring`.
+--- Decides how to format items when displayed in the picker.
+--- (default: `tostring`)
 ---@field format_item? fun(item: any):string
 ---
---- Function to preview an individual item from `items`.
---- If missing, no special preview is used.
---- Should ensure a buffer with contents (text, highlighting) providing details about an item.
---- Should return a table with information that `vim.ui.select` implementations may
---- use to show the preview buffer in the way they see fit:
----     - {buf}? (`integer`) - buffer id with preview. If missing, no preview should be shown.
----     - {pos}? (`[integer, integer]`) - (1,0)-indexed tuple of where to position cursor
----       in the preview buffer. If missing, should be treated as `{ 1, 0 }`.
----@field preview_item? fun(item: any):table
+--- Decides how to preview an item by preparing a scratch buffer with the item details (including text, highlighting, etc.).
+--- When the picker decides to "preview" an item, it should call this function.
+--- Must return a table with these keys:
+---     - {buf}? (`integer`) Buffer containing the previewed item details, or nil if no preview should be shown.
+---     - {pos}? (`[integer, integer]`) Specifies the (1,0)-indexed cursor position in the preview buffer. If nil, should be treated as `{ 1, 0 }`.
+---     - {pos_end}? (`[integer, integer]`) Specifies the (1,0)-indexed (end-exclusive) end position of the preview range. If nil, no range is intended for a preview.
+---@field preview_item? fun(item: any):{buf?:integer, pos?:[integer,integer]}

comment_regular_data.body: Perhaps it would be better to place `@inlinedoc` at the usage rather than at the definition, as in this PR. Currently, there are several `lsp.*` types mentioned in the documentation, but their definitions cannot be found, such as `lsp.ReferenceContext?` in `:h vim.lsp.buf.references()`.

> Perhaps it would be better to place `@inlinedoc` at the usage rather than at the definition

worth exploring in a separate pr!

title: docs: lsp.CodeActionContext, nested `@inlinedoc`
